reason: Irreversible-op protocol: archive, dry-run, second reviewer, concrete post-verify check.
Standard git conventions a capable agent already follows; no org-specific value added.
Specific tone fences plus explicit manual exit review matched to judgment-based task.
Org-specific flag facts (registry, naming, kill switches) with concrete correctness checklist.
High-stakes audit constraints with narrow waiver exception path and concrete post-check.
Forces rigid sequential authoring order for a doc; justification is asserted, not real.
Concrete org dashboard conventions with cold-open verification checklist against each rule.
Machine-critical strict format with silent failures narrated as prose, no validator shipped.
Naming rules explicitly matched to low-stakes review-time catch; manual check justified.
Mandates impact matrix, screenshots, self-review transcript on every PR, even one-liners.
Ships field-aware scrubber plus independent verifier script; real exit check for PII leaks.
Format/sourcing rules fine but ends in vague 'quality' fluff, no concrete exit check.
Explicitly separates machine-checkable calculations from human judgment calls, with declared checks.
Generic JSON formatting a base agent already does by default; no real gap.
Artifact order only (not authoring order), justified by downstream CI parsing, concrete exit.
Org-specific LDAP topology facts with verifiable exit check against IAM portal.
Concrete paging thresholds and escalation tiers with a numeric audit exit check.
Fixed sequence justified by real engineering practice (clean commits, valid test runs).
